violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Peppers and onions were observed held at 108 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

The establishment is using a special process for bottling juice, without approval from a process authority and the RIDOH. At the time of inspection, PIC voluntarily disposed of bottled juices. The establishment must get all processes approved from a process authority and obtain approval from the RIDOH-Center for Food Protection prior to selling the above-mentioned products. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Cod was observed thawing on the shelf next to 3 bay sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Cod was at 31 degrees Farenheit and was moved to refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Working containers of sugar and spices are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Cookies packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

The door to the outside, located in the entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
Oil was observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and hallway. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Inspector observed refrigerator fan unit to be dripping water onto lids of containers which had plastic wrap underneath. PIC changed all contaminated lids, plastic wrap and was moved. PIC called for service prior to inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
The faucet located at the 3 bay sink was not in good repair, observed leak. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of food residue and other debris. 1. Mop sink 2. Stove (between equipment) 3. Oven handles New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
